intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (Yellow Pot) is empty/barren. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling sprout/stem. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor and a single leaf specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Systemic Loss (Original specimens absent; single unidentified sprout remains)

plant_audit.P1: No visible plant matter. Soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2/P4: Original Mexican Mint/Silver Guest absent. A single, thin, pale green stem/sprout is present, showing signs of etiolation.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor is stable. A single, broad, dark green leaf is visible, appearing healthy but isolated.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments observed in P2/P4. Electronic component (header pin) observed on the desk surface in early images, removed in later images.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Stable. The sprout in P2/P4 shows slight postural change, leaning toward the light source.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant loss of visual detail due to lighting; sprout in P2/P4 appears to have lost its upper leaf structure, leaving only the stem.
temporal_deltas.CURRENT: The sprout in P2/P4 is now reduced to a small, vertical green sliver, indicating severe decline or potential dormancy/death.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. P1 is effectively a dead zone. P2/P4 is failing, with the specimen likely unable to sustain itself. P3 is the only stable element, though it lacks the expected density of a Pothos plant.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P2/P4 (likely a calcium supplement attempt). Removal of electronic component from the desk surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the biome. My first action was to map the current physical state against the registry, identifying significant systemic losses. I then validated these findings by tracking the degradation of the P2/P4 sprout across the five-day sequence. The biome is currently under-populated and struggling; the lack of light and potential nutrient issues have led to the loss of most registered specimens. The remaining sprout in P2/P4 is in a terminal state of etiolation.
